title: Richly Bitter French Apple Tarte Tatin
description: A classic French dessert with a twist, featuring bitter apples and a soured cream and chive dip, perfect for satisfying midnight cravings. This low-glycemic-friendly dish is sure to delight.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat and add the apples, cooking until they are tender and lightly browned.
In a separate bowl, mix together the soured cream, chives, flour, sugar, eggs, and salt.
Roll out the puff pastry and place it over the apple mixture, tucking the edges under the apples.
Bake the tarte tatin for 25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
Remove the tarte tatin from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Invert the tarte tatin onto a serving plate and serve warm with the soured cream and chive dip.
Refrigerate the tarte tatin for at least 30 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
